description: SEC EDGAR fundamentals fallback when Yahoo data is empty — GitHub trovlunde/stock_analyzer#8

userStories.title: Fundamentals provider protocol and YFinance adapter
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a typed fundamentals provider with a YFinance-backed adapter so EDGAR fallback can plug in without scattering yfinance calls.
acceptanceCriteria: New fundamentals provider module defines a protocol (or ABC) for annual and quarterly income-statement DataFrames
YFinance adapter delegates to existing yfinance Ticker `.financials` and `.quarterly_financials` behaviour
Provider returns pandas DataFrames in the same orientation callers expect (line items as index, periods as columns)
No network calls in unit tests — adapter tested with a mocked Ticker/fixture
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: EDGAR fallback adapter via edgartools
userStories.description: As a fundamental-analysis user, I want SEC EDGAR statements when Yahoo returns empty financials so valuation and fin-statement classifiers have data for more tickers.
acceptanceCriteria: `edgartools` added via `uv add`; importable in CI without network

EDGAR adapter fetches authoritative statements and maps to minimum line items used downstream (e.g. Total Revenue, Net Income, EBITDA, EBIT, Gross Profit, Operating Income, Cost Of Revenue, Depreciation)
Adapter is only invoked when YFinance annual or quarterly financials are empty
EDGAR fetch path is mockable — no live SEC HTTP in pytest
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: Cache EDGAR payloads in SqlCacheStore
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want EDGAR responses cached in the existing SQLite cache store with namespaced keys so repeat lookups avoid SEC rate limits.
acceptanceCriteria: EDGAR adapter reads/writes via `get_cache_store()` using keys like `edgar:{ticker}:{statement_kind}`
Cache hit returns deserialized DataFrame without refetching
Cache miss fetches (or uses mock), then `put`s payload
Round-trip test uses in-memory SQLite fixture (pattern from existing cache store tests)
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: Composite provider behind get_ticker_financials surface
userStories.description: As a developer, I want YFinance-primary + EDGAR-fallback wired through existing helper entry points so valuation and classifier callers do not branch on provider details.
acceptanceCriteria: Composite provider tries YFinance first; falls back to cached EDGAR only when annual or quarterly financials are empty
`get_ticker_financials(ticker)` uses the composite provider for annual statements
Helpers expose quarterly financials (and balance sheet if needed by fin-statement classifier) through the same provider surface — callers stop reaching directly into raw yfinance for statements
yfinance remains the primary price/history source — no paid market-data API introduced
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: P2 pytest: empty Yahoo mock triggers EDGAR fallback
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a regression test proving the fallback populates minimum statement fields when Yahoo mocks return empty DataFrames.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: YFinance mock returns empty annual and quarterly financials → composite provider returns non-empty DataFrame with required line items from EDGAR mock
Test asserts cache key is written on miss and read on subsequent call
No network access in CI
uv run pytest passes
